⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 U.S. Energy Secretary Wright said that gas under $3 a gallon by the Summer is an aggressive timeline. Interviewer: "You've sounded more optimistic in past interviews saying you expect gas prices to ease and that there's a good chance that prices could hit under $3 a gallon by the summer. Is that still your belief, particularly given today's blockade in the strait, or has the dynamic changed there?" Wright: "Well, by the summer is an aggressive time frame now. You know, going into this, this is a forty-seven-year-long conflict with Iran that's repeatedly not only been a threat to American soldiers and peace in the Middle East, but also a threat to energy supplies." @wfwitness

⚡ 🇫🇷 🇸🇾 Reuters: A French court has found cement company Lafarge guilty of financing jihadist groups in Syria, including ISIS and the Nusra Front, to maintain operations during the civil war. The court ruled that Lafarge paid approximately €5.6 million between 2013 and 2014 to armed groups, marking the first time a company has been convicted in France for financing terrorism. Several former executives, including ex-CEO Bruno Lafont, were also convicted, with Lafont sentenced to six years in prison. @wfwitness

⚡ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 ✍️ The Blockade of Hormuz or the strangling of Iran. After an extensive bombing campaign which mostly failed to achieve any strategic goal, the United States is now resorting to its naval power to close the strait of Hormuz in an attempt to gain leverage on Iran. While Iran's anti-ship missiles, drones and mines have halted oil export from the Gulf state, it was able to export some of its own petroleum to generate valuable revenue. The US has now chosen that cutting Iran's financial lifeline will be worth the negative impact on the global economy. A prolonged blockade can effectively destroy the Iranian economy as a whole, greatly reducing the state power and ability to wage war if the country does not fall into civil war. However if Iran thinks that it won't be able to use the strait anymore there is now nothing holding them back from saturating Hormuz with naval mines, closing it for months, and for everyone, even if the blockade is lifted. This is probably Iran's last and most powerful card left. Iranian tankers could still escape seizure by the US Navy by staying in Iranian then Pakistani territorial waters. @wfwitness

🇺🇸 🇧🇷 Brazil’s fugitive former intelligence chief and lawmaker Alexandre Ramagem has been taken into custody by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement. Ramagem, who faces a 16-year prison sentence in Brazil for his alleged involvement in a coup plot with far-right ex-President Jair Bolsonaro, has been living in exile in Florida in recent months. Journalist Paulo Figueiredo, an ally of Bolsonaro, said Ramagem was detained after a traffic stop and subsequently referred to ICE. He added that Ramagem has legal status in the US and has a pending asylum request under review. Figueiredo told CNN that Immigrex, a company where he is a partner, is providing legal representation to Ramagem. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

↩️ Reply to DD Geopolitics: "🇺🇸 The USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) has crossed the Strait of Gibraltar. It is now the third U.S. aircraft carrier entering the Iran theater. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics | Socials | Donate | Advertising" 🇺🇸 According to USNI News—the media arm of the U.S. Naval Institute—the USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) is operating off Namibia, taking an unusual route around Africa to join a growing U.S. naval buildup in the Arabian Sea amid a U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Instead of transiting the Strait of Gibraltar and Mediterranean, the strike group—escorted by USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Mason (DDG-87), USS Ross (DDG-71), and supported by USNS Arctic (TAOE-8)—is bypassing the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb. ℹ️ Earlier today, some reports said it had crossed Gibraltar; however, this now appears not to be the case. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness ⚡ 🇮🇷 The number of ships transits between Kharg island and mainland Iran has remained high despite the ongoing ceasefire. Satellite imagery from April 6th and 11th showed 7 and 28 ships respectively. This is down from the peak of the war (33) but still significantly above usual numbers. The IRGC is expected to continue the renforcement of the island as the threat of the return of the war looms over the region. This is not the daily number of transits but the number of ships seen at once on the satellite imagery . @wfwitness

Bush didn’t sail through the Strait of Gibraltar to the Mediterranean, but rather the aircraft carrier will sail around Africa to avoid sailing through the Red Sea and the Bab el-Mandeb. Both of these areas would put aircraft carrier at risk of attack from Houthi drones or missiles. A notice to mariners states that a “grace period” allowing neutral vessels to leave Iranian ports has ended, warning that any ship entering or departing the blockaded area without authorisation may face interception, diversion, or capture. @wfwitness

⚡ 🇺🇸 The American carrier strike group centered around USS Gerald R. Ford was operating today east of Cyprus. She was already in the same area two days ago, she is not in transit and is expected to remain in the area until new orders are given. The carrier can be seen being refuelled by a supply ship. They are flanked by a pair of Arleigh Burke class destroyers. The European Space Agency providing the satellite imagery is delaying by a day the release of satellite imagery of the eastern Mediterranean sea where the European carrier group is operating. The American carrier strike group was caught here right on the edge. This is why the right part of the satellite view isn't loaded. CVN-78 33.75N 29.18E @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 WSJ: The US blockade of Iranian trade through the Strait of Hormuz is pushing global oil markets into renewed turmoil, with supply already under severe strain. Around “2 million barrels per day” of Iranian oil exports could be removed from the market, intensifying a global scramble for crude. Oil prices have surged, with Brent crude rising to about $101 a barrel. Shipbrokers say that it isn't clear how ships can satisfy both US and Iranian conditions on leaving the Persian Gulf, and tanker owners say they will hesitate before sending new vessels through the Strait @wfwitness

🇺🇸 💬 🇮🇷 CNN: Trump officials are internally discussing details for a potential second, in-person meeting with Iranian officials before the ceasefire between Washington and Tehran expires next week, should the opportunity present itself, a source familiar with the talks told CNN, though it’s unclear whether such a meeting could materialize. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇱🇧 Hezbollah Secretary-General Sheikh Naim Qassem on attempts to create sectarian strife: "They are starting to promote that Turkey is creating a problem between Sunnis and Shia. Who is working on this? The Americans and the Israelis and Israel's servants internally. They want the strife to be between others, so they can get rid of both. We with our brothers the Sunnis are one heart. This strife, they will not succeed in it. Israel's servants will fail. We will not allow strife to raise its head. The Christians of all their sects and the Muslims of all their denominations — Sunnis and Druze and Alawites and all — are our brothers in the nation, in religion, and in the heavenly messages. What unites us is much stronger than the devils of the interior and exterior and the trumpets of strife." 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
